sourceMovie.title: A Perfect Ending
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto A Perfect Ending (2012) as a drama theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The drama and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: My Policeman
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If A Perfect Ending clicked for you, My Policeman (2022) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and romance, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Catch and Release
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If A Perfect Ending clicked for you, Catch and Release (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and romance, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after A Perfect Ending?
faq.answer: Try My Policeman, Catch and Release, Lost and Delirious—each shares DNA with A Perfect Ending while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is A Perfect Ending?
faq.answer: A Perfect Ending (2012) is best described as Drama, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is A Perfect Ending worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
